vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.daily
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.daily, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.daily
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.daily/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: File permissions changes are explicitly marked as safe_to_remediate in the policy. This finding involves setting permissions on /etc/cron.daily, a standard hardening measure with low risk of system breakage or lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; potential misapplication could affect cron job execution
